name: CheckboxGroup
description: A container that provides shared state to a series of checkboxes, managing a value array of checked item names. Supports a parent checkbox for select-all behavior. Built on Base UI CheckboxGroup with StyleX styling.

Root.description: The group container — renders a <div> that provides CheckboxGroupContext to child Checkbox.Root components.

value.description: Names of the checkboxes that should be ticked. Use for controlled mode.

defaultValue.description: Names of the checkboxes that should be initially ticked. Use for uncontrolled mode.

onValueChange.description: Callback fired when a checkbox in the group is ticked or unticked.

allValues.description: Names of all checkboxes in the group. Required when using a parent checkbox for select-all behavior.

disabled.description: Whether all checkboxes in the group should ignore user interaction.

dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the group is disabled.

intents.reasoning: CheckboxGroup manages shared state across multiple checkboxes, tracking which items are selected via a value array.
intents.composition:
<CheckboxGroup.Root defaultValue={['a']}>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root name="a">
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Option A
  </label>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root name="b">
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Option B
  </label>
</CheckboxGroup.Root>

intents.reasoning: A parent checkbox inside a controlled CheckboxGroup auto-derives its checked/indeterminate state from children, providing a native select-all pattern. The group MUST be controlled (value + onValueChange) for the parent checkbox to work.
intents.composition:
const [value, setValue] = useState<string[]>([]);

<CheckboxGroup.Root allValues={['a', 'b', 'c']} value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root parent>
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Select all
  </label>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root name="a">
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Option A
  </label>
</CheckboxGroup.Root>

avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Checkbox alone. CheckboxGroup adds overhead for managing a value array that isn't needed for a single toggle.

avoidWhen.scenario: Mutually exclusive options where only one can be selected
avoidWhen.reasoning: Checkboxes allow multiple selections. Use Radio or RadioGroup for single-select.

examples.name: basic-group
examples.description: Basic checkbox group with shared state

examples.name: select-all
examples.description: Parent checkbox for select-all behavior (requires controlled state)
examples.code:
const [value, setValue] = useState<string[]>([]);

<CheckboxGroup.Root allValues={['a', 'b', 'c']} value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root parent>
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Select all
  </label>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root name="a">
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Item A
  </label>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root name="b">
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Item B
  </label>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root name="c">
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Item C
  </label>
</CheckboxGroup.Root>

examples.name: disabled-group
examples.code:
<CheckboxGroup.Root disabled defaultValue={['a']}>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root name="a">
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Item A (checked)
  </label>
  <label>
    <Checkbox.Root name="b">
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    Item B
  </label>
</CheckboxGroup.Root>
